description.p: Whisp is a gesture-driven note-taking application designed for GNOME.

ul.li: Live Markdown formatting (bold, italics, headers, code, lists)
Swipe navigation between notes
Searchable note list directly from the header
Instant saving and Native UI styling

screenshot.caption: Pin important notes and format technical snippets with ease
Stay organized with tiered list management
Rich WYSIWYG markdown support with formatted links

description.p: Hotfix: Resolved an issue where the Donate button in the changelog dialog would not open the browser.

p.em: More Commands and Quality of Life

description.p: This release brings a highly requested formatting toggle, a massive expansion to the command engine, and several critical bug fixes.

ul.li: Per-Note WYSIWYG: You can now toggle WYSIWYG formatting individually for each note, giving you finer control over your workspace.

More Smart Expansions: The command engine has been expanded! Added new commands to instantly fetch random quotes, Wikipedia summaries, secure passwords, and UUIDs. We also added text-manipulation commands like uppercase , lowercase, prepend , append and formats like sentence case and title case.
System Locale Support: Dates and times generated by expansions (`::date`, `::time`, `::timestamp`) now intelligently respect your global OS Region and Language format settings.
Command Documentation: To see a full cheat-sheet of all available smart expansions and commands, please check out the newly updated Manual page on our project website!
Added a small option for toggling smart command confirmation toasts in preferences.
Auto-scroll: Injecting large text blocks via commands will now smoothly auto-scroll the editor down to your cursor's new position.
Fixed an issue where the autocomplete dropdown window would get cut off if the app window was too narrow.
Fixed an encoding issue preventing certain Spanish symbols from rendering correctly.
Squashed the dead letter bug and resolved a visual glitch that caused the first note to flash when opening the app.

description.p: Donations are now live! Whisp is designed and developed by a single student. If this app improves your daily workflow, please consider supporting its continued development via the project website. Your support means the world!

ul.li: Smart Text Expansions: Type `::` to open a native autocomplete dropdown and instantly insert dates (`::today`, `::tomorrow`), or times (`::now`).
Data Generators: Added built-in generators to roll dice (`::roll(d20)`, `::roll(4d6)`) or insert random strings and numbers (`::random(str, 20)`).
Smart Argument Filling: Selecting complex commands like `::roll(20)` from the dropdown will automatically highlight the arguments so you can instantly overwrite them.
Added background running permission and start-on-startup capability to pave the way for future features (This is completely opt-in and toggled off by default).
Fixed an issue where the pin icon was not appearing correctly on some KDE devices.
Revamped the entire website to look more professional and help users easily identify features and differences compared to similar apps.
Added documentation for the new expansion functions to the user manual page.
Updated the Flathub manifest with the new Donate button and Contact links.

description.p: Added clickable links, new keyboard shortcuts, and bug fixes.

ul.li: Clickable Links: You can now Ctrl+Click on any URL in your note to instantly open it in your default web browser.

Power User Shortcuts: Added new global keyboard shortcuts! You can now Pin Notes (Ctrl+Shift+P), Move Notes to Front (Ctrl+Shift+M), Copy Entire Note (Ctrl+Shift+C), and quickly jump to your First/Last note (Alt+F / Alt+L).
Shortcut Documentation: All new keybinds have been properly added and documented inside the built-in Keyboard Shortcuts help window.
Bug Fix: Fixed a logic bug that could occasionally cause accidentally deleted notes to save themselves as ghost notes.

description.p: Added note pinning, list management, and UI improvements.

ul.li: Pin Notes: You can now pin important notes to the front of the carousel so they are always exactly where you need them. Pinned notes bypass the auto-archive timer and persist across app restarts.

Tree-Aware List Reordering: Organize your thoughts effortlessly! You can now use Alt+Up/Down to dynamically reorder list items. It's fully tree-aware, meaning you can swap siblings and perfectly preserve the hierarchy and nested children.
UI Modernization: Replaced legacy dialog boxes with sleek, modern Libadwaita Alert Dialogs and updated UI assets across the application. Thank you to the community for this PR!
Bug Fix: Removed an overly aggressive check that was accidentally deleting notes if they started with the word "list".

description.p: Added organization, cleanup, and customization features.

ul.li: Auto-Archive: Added a new setting to automatically hide inactive notes after a set period (1 week, 1 month, etc.) without deleting them. Older notes remain fully searchable via Ctrl+F.
Max Carousel Size: Keep your desk clean by strictly limiting the carousel to your 10, 25, or 50 most recent notes.
Preferences Overhaul: Completely redesigned the Preferences window with modern Libadwaita tabs (Appearance, Behavior, Storage) to make customizing Whisp easier than ever.
Bug Fix: Fixed an issue where raw backticks were improperly rendered around inline code snippets while WYSIWYG mode was active.

description.p: Added new search capabilities and UX improvements.

ul.li: In-Note Search: Press Ctrl+F to open the new search bar. It highlights all matching words and allows you to seamlessly jump between them. A huge thank you to @carlosjdelgado for building this incredible feature!
What's New Dialog: Added a beautiful dedicated changelog popup to keep you informed about new features after an update.
Cleaner Deletions: The Delete Note confirmation dialog now intelligently truncates long note titles so it always stays small and clean.

description.p: Added an Interactive Welcome Note for first-time onboarding to help users discover shortcuts and features.
Added a new visual Appearance selector and new Paper Themes:

ul.li: Replaced the basic dropdown menu with a visual Grid selector for Paper Themes, showing live miniature snippets of how your notes will look.
Added a new 'Large Grid' paper background option.
Added a visual Theme Switcher in the main menu to easily toggle between System, Light, and Dark modes.
Added a Startup Behavior preference to choose whether the app restores your last active note or starts with a fresh empty draft.
Added an interactive 'Undo' button directly to the note deletion popup toast for immediate and reliable recovery.
Fixed a race condition where quickly deleting a note using a keyboard shortcut would occasionally cause the note to save itself in the background as a "ghost note".

description.p: Added new features and fixed bugs:

ul.li: Added a visual Theme Switcher in the main menu to easily toggle between System, Light, and Dark modes.
Added a Startup Behavior preference to choose whether the app restores your last active note or starts with a fresh empty draft.
Fixed a race condition where quickly deleting a note using a keyboard shortcut would occasionally cause the note to save itself in the background as a "ghost note".

description.p: Fixed a severe Flatpak Document Portal permission error that caused permanent deletion when using custom Note directories.
Added delete confirmation dialog. Fixed a bug where deleting empty notes could lock the UI. Restored deleted notes to their original position.
Added a native GNOME About dialog with credits and license details. Restored original icon size based on user feedback.
Metadata updates to satisfy Flathub quality guidelines and roadmap additions.
Initial release of Whisp. A minimalist, gesture-driven scratchpad for GNOME.
